title: Sarcasm and Irony in English
description: Spotting and using dry humor, sarcasm, and situational irony in everyday speech.
summary_pt_br: Sarcasmo: dizer o oposto do que pensa, geralmente em tom seco. Ironia: realidade contradiz expectativa.

scenes.subtitle_en: Spotting and using dry humor, sarcasm, and situational irony in everyday speech.
scenes.subtitle_pt: Sarcasmo: dizer o oposto do que pensa, geralmente em tom seco. Ironia: realidade contradiz expectativa.

scenes.text_en: Spotting and using dry humor, sarcasm, and situational irony in everyday speech. Today's focus: sarcasm and irony in english. 'Oh great, another rainy Monday.' — classic sarcasm.
scenes.text_pt: Sarcasmo: dizer o oposto do que pensa, geralmente em tom seco. Ironia: realidade contradiz expectativa. Foco de hoje: sarcasm and irony in english. 'Ótimo, outra segunda chuvosa.' Sarcasmo clássico.

scenes.title: Sarcasm and Irony in English
scenes.body_en: Sarcasm: saying the opposite of what you mean, usually in a flat tone. Irony: reality contradicts expectation. British and American humor differ in register and bite.
scenes.body_pt: Sarcasmo: dizer o oposto do que pensa, geralmente em tom seco. Ironia: realidade contradiz expectativa.

examples.en: 'Oh great, another rainy Monday.' — classic sarcasm.
The irony is that she hated coffee and now owns a café.
examples.pt: A ironia é que ela odiava café e hoje tem cafeteria.

examples.en: I LOVE standing in line for two hours.
examples.pt: ADORO ficar duas horas na fila. (sarcasmo)

scenes.title: Key vocabulary — Sarcasm and Irony in English

exercise.question: Complete: "'Oh great, ___ rainy Monday.' — classic sarcasm."
exercise.explanation: Full sentence: "'Oh great, another rainy Monday.' — classic sarcasm."

exercise.question: Which sentence is correct?
options: I haven't heard anything about that.
The irony is that she hated coffee and now owns a café.
exercise.explanation: The correct sentence is: "The irony is that she hated coffee and now owns a café."
exercise.hint_pt_br: A ironia é que ela odiava café e hoje tem cafeteria.

exercise.explanation: Five key terms from the lesson. Match each English word to its Portuguese meaning.

scenes.title: Sarcasm and Irony in English — dialog
scenes.pt_summary: Diálogo sobre sarcasm and irony in english. Pratique as frases marcadas.

turns.speaker: ai
turns.text: My flight got cancelled for the third time today.

turns.speaker: user
turns.target: Oh wonderful, your favorite kind of vacation.

turns.speaker: ai
turns.text: Tell me an irony from your week.

turns.speaker: user
turns.target: The coffee shop owner forgot to order coffee.

turns.speaker: ai
turns.text: Is American humor more direct than British?

turns.speaker: user
turns.target: Usually — British is drier, more understated.

turns.speaker: ai
turns.text: Humor in a second language is the final frontier of fluency.

scenes.title: The comedy of misfortune
scenes.pt_summary: Ouça com atenção. Depois, escreva em inglês o que entendeu — resumo, detalhes e sua opinião. O professor vai ler e comentar.
scenes.prompt_en: Describe one ironic situation you witnessed recently, using sarcasm sparingly.
scenes.prompt_pt: Escreva em inglês o que ouviu. Resumo curto com quem falou, o que disseram e sua opinião.

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.en: At the food truck on a particularly rainy Tuesday, Rafa was entertaining regulars with stories from the week.

paragraphs.speaker: Rafa
paragraphs.en: Oh wonderful, the only day I forget my umbrella is the day it rains for six straight hours.

paragraphs.speaker: Customer
paragraphs.en: The irony is that the coffee shop next door ran out of coffee this morning.

paragraphs.speaker: Rafa
paragraphs.en: Beautiful irony, really. Life writes far better comedy than any screenwriter ever could.

paragraphs.speaker: Customer
paragraphs.en: British humor survives on irony. American humor charges straight in, like a friendly bulldozer.

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.en: Humor in English, Rafa said, was the final exam — and almost nobody took it seriously enough to pass.

scenes.title: Recap reading
scenes.passage_en: At Amazing School, the class just finished "Sarcasm and Irony in English". Spotting and using dry humor, sarcasm, and situational irony in everyday speech. 'Oh great, another rainy Monday.' — classic sarcasm. The irony is that she hated coffee and now owns a café. Mr. Kumar reminded the students that practice is the key — listen, read aloud, write your own examples. Every small step adds up.

check.question: According to Mr. Kumar, what is the key to learning?
check.explanation: Practice — listen, read aloud, write your own examples.
check.hint_pt_br: Prática: ouvir, ler em voz alta, escrever exemplos.

scenes.title: Listen to Bia
scenes.audio_text_en: Hello! I'm Bia, from Praia do Sol. Today in class we studied "Sarcasm and Irony in English". I liked it because it was clear and useful. See you next time!

check.question: What did Bia study today?
options: A grammar rule from last year
check.explanation: She says: "Sarcasm and Irony in English".
check.hint_pt_br: Ela fala o título: "Sarcasm and Irony in English".
